nodes.label: AI Infrastructure Buildout
nodes.plain_english_description: Hyperscalers and enterprises are investing record amounts in AI compute infrastructure, creating sustained demand across power, cooling, networking, and data-centre real estate.

nodes.label: AI Compute Demand
nodes.plain_english_description: Demand for AI training and inference compute is expanding the GPU, networking, and accelerator market.

nodes.label: Geopolitical Risk Rising
nodes.plain_english_description: Rising geopolitical tensions, NATO budget commitments, and military conflict are driving defence spending growth globally.

nodes.label: GLP-1 Drug Adoption
nodes.plain_english_description: Obesity and diabetes treatment is being transformed by GLP-1 receptor agonists (Ozempic, Zepbound), with rapidly expanding prescription volumes, manufacturing constraints, and downstream economic consequences.

nodes.label: Domestic Manufacturing Investment
nodes.plain_english_description: US CHIPS Act subsidies, tariff-driven reshoring, and supply-chain security concerns are directing record capital into domestic semiconductor fabs and industrial facilities.

nodes.label: Falling Yields / Mortgage Relief
nodes.plain_english_description: Declining bond yields and mortgage rates improve housing affordability, stimulate buyer demand, and re-open the refinance market.

nodes.label: Water Scarcity & Infrastructure Stress
nodes.plain_english_description: Aging water infrastructure, drought cycles, and population growth are forcing municipal and industrial water system investment.

nodes.label: Grid Electrification & EV Adoption
nodes.plain_english_description: The global transition to electric vehicles, renewable energy, and grid modernisation is creating multi-decade copper and critical mineral demand growth.

nodes.label: Central Bank Gold Demand
nodes.plain_english_description: Central banks, particularly in emerging markets, are diversifying reserves away from USD assets by buying gold at record pace.

nodes.label: Crypto Liquidity Cycle
nodes.plain_english_description: Spot Bitcoin ETF access, institutional adoption, and exchange volume cycles are driving Bitcoin price exposure and crypto infrastructure revenue.

nodes.label: AI Energy & Nuclear Revival
nodes.plain_english_description: AI data-centre power demand is activating nuclear, grid equipment, cooling, uranium, and enrichment supply chains — the only path to reliable 24/7 carbon-free baseload at scale.
nodes.risk_note: Valuation crowding risk; hyperscaler capex guidance cuts would challenge this thesis.

nodes.label: GLP-1 and Metabolic Health
nodes.plain_english_description: GLP-1 adoption is disrupting pharmaceuticals, medical devices, healthcare payers, and consumer food — creating direct beneficiaries, supply-chain names, and negative-exposure watches simultaneously.
nodes.risk_note: Drug safety concerns, payer coverage restrictions, or competitive next-gen failures are key risks.

nodes.label: Defence Rearmament & Security
nodes.plain_english_description: Rising defence budgets, NATO commitments, and geopolitical conflict are creating structural order backlog growth for prime contractors, missile systems, drones, and naval shipbuilding.
nodes.risk_note: Budget cuts or peace resolution are the primary risks.

nodes.label: Cybersecurity & Digital Resilience
nodes.plain_english_description: Rising breach costs, AI governance risk, identity attacks, and cloud migration are driving board-level security platform spend and zero-trust adoption.
nodes.risk_note: Platform consolidation may benefit leaders and pressure smaller vendors simultaneously.

nodes.label: Reshoring & Industrial Capex
nodes.plain_english_description: CHIPS Act subsidies, tariff policy, and supply-chain security are directing record industrial capex into semiconductor fabs, factory automation, and electrical infrastructure.
nodes.risk_note: CHIPS Act funding delays or trade-policy reversals are primary risks.

nodes.label: Housing & Rate Sensitivity
nodes.plain_english_description: Mortgage rates are the primary lever for US housing affordability. Rate relief unlocks buyer demand, homebuilder orders, building products, and mortgage services simultaneously.
nodes.risk_note: Rate trajectory reversal, credit conditions tightening, or regional supply imbalances are key risks.

nodes.label: Water Infrastructure
nodes.plain_english_description: Aging US water systems, drought risk, and industrial water treatment needs are driving regulated utility rate cases, infrastructure spending, and water technology adoption.
nodes.risk_note: Regulatory rate-case delays and municipal budget constraints are primary risks.

nodes.label: Critical Minerals & Copper
nodes.plain_english_description: Grid expansion, EV adoption, AI data-centre buildout, and supply-chain nationalisation are creating structural copper and critical mineral demand — separate from short-term commodity cycles.
nodes.risk_note: China economic slowdown, supply additions, or EV demand disappointment are primary risks.

nodes.label: Gold & Real Assets
nodes.plain_english_description: Central bank diversification, real-rate expectations, geopolitical risk, and USD reserve concerns are driving gold demand — creating a multi-year structural bid separate from inflation alone.
nodes.risk_note: Real rate increases or USD strength are the primary gold headwinds.

nodes.label: Digital Assets Infrastructure
nodes.plain_english_description: Spot Bitcoin ETF approval, institutional adoption, and exchange revenue cycles are creating investable equity exposure to crypto infrastructure without direct coin ownership.
nodes.risk_note: Regulatory crackdown, exchange failures, or bear-market volume collapse are primary risks.

nodes.label: Nuclear Power Revival
nodes.plain_english_description: Reliable baseload power demand for AI data centres is accelerating nuclear restart, new SMR deployment, and reactor fuel supply chains.

nodes.label: Data Centre Power Infrastructure
nodes.plain_english_description: Power management, cooling, and electrical equipment demand is growing directly with data-centre capacity expansion.

nodes.label: Uranium Supply Chain
nodes.plain_english_description: Nuclear power expansion requires uranium mining, conversion, and enrichment — a supply chain with multi-year lead times.

nodes.label: AI Accelerators & Networking
nodes.plain_english_description: GPU accelerators, custom ASICs, and data-centre networking are the direct compute layer of AI infrastructure.

nodes.label: Direct GLP-1 Drug Revenue
nodes.plain_english_description: Companies generating revenue directly from approved GLP-1 drugs for obesity and diabetes management.

nodes.label: GLP-1 Manufacturing Supply Chain
nodes.plain_english_description: Peptide API synthesis, fill-finish capacity, and injectable delivery system manufacturing required to scale GLP-1 production.

nodes.label: Metabolic Diagnostics & Payers
nodes.plain_english_description: Diagnostic testing, monitoring devices, and healthcare payers face mixed exposure as GLP-1 adoption changes metabolic disease management.

nodes.label: Consumer Appetite Impact
nodes.plain_english_description: GLP-1 appetite reduction creates volume headwinds for calorie-dense food and beverage companies.

nodes.label: Defence Hardware & Platforms
nodes.plain_english_description: Prime contractors, missiles, air defence, shipbuilding, and aircraft serving government defence budgets.

nodes.label: Drones, Autonomy & Defence Tech
nodes.plain_english_description: Unmanned systems, autonomous platforms, defence IT, and space/reconnaissance technology with catalyst-driven demand.

nodes.label: Security Platform & Endpoint
nodes.plain_english_description: Enterprise security platform providers with endpoint, network, and cloud protection capabilities.

nodes.label: Zero Trust & Cloud Security
nodes.plain_english_description: SASE, zero-trust network access, and cloud-native security platforms driven by cloud migration and remote-work security mandates.

nodes.label: Semiconductor Fab Investment
nodes.plain_english_description: CHIPS Act-funded and tariff-driven US semiconductor fab construction driving equipment, materials, and construction spend.

nodes.label: Industrial Construction & Capex
nodes.plain_english_description: Factory automation, engineering and construction, and industrial distribution benefiting from domestic manufacturing investment.

nodes.label: Homebuilder Demand Cycle
nodes.plain_english_description: Homebuilder order rates are directly sensitive to mortgage rate changes that shift buyer affordability and qualification pools.

nodes.label: Housing Supply Chain & Services
nodes.plain_english_description: Building products, home improvement retail, and mortgage services that benefit from housing transaction and construction volumes.

nodes.label: Water Utility Infrastructure
nodes.plain_english_description: Regulated water utilities invest in infrastructure upgrades and recover costs through rate-case filings.

nodes.label: Water Technology & Treatment Systems
nodes.plain_english_description: Water treatment, pumps, valves, meters, and testing systems addressing infrastructure upgrades and industrial water efficiency.

nodes.label: Copper & Electrification Demand
nodes.plain_english_description: Copper demand is structurally growing across grid expansion, EV adoption, and data-centre infrastructure — independent of short-cycle commodity volatility.

nodes.label: Battery Materials Supply
nodes.plain_english_description: Lithium and rare earth materials are critical for EV batteries and energy storage, with supply concentrated in geopolitically sensitive regions.

nodes.label: Gold Safe-Haven & Reserve Demand
nodes.plain_english_description: Central bank buying, real-rate expectations, and geopolitical risk are structural gold demand drivers distinct from inflation.

nodes.label: Gold Mining Operations
nodes.plain_english_description: Senior gold miners and royalty streamers provide leveraged equity exposure to gold price appreciation through operational and financial leverage.

nodes.label: Spot Bitcoin ETP Access
nodes.plain_english_description: SEC-approved spot Bitcoin ETPs provide direct Bitcoin price exposure in a regulated equity wrapper.

nodes.label: Crypto Exchange & Mining Infrastructure
nodes.plain_english_description: Crypto exchange revenue, corporate Bitcoin exposure, and mining economics create equity expressions of the crypto liquidity cycle.

nodes.label: AI Compute Accelerators & Networking
nodes.plain_english_description: GPUs, custom AI ASICs, and data-centre Ethernet switches that form the compute layer of AI infrastructure.
nodes.risk_note: Export controls, competitive pressure, and valuation crowding are key risks.

nodes.label: Power Management & Electrical Equipment
nodes.plain_english_description: Power management systems, switchgear, transformers, and cooling infrastructure for data centres and the power grid.
nodes.risk_note: Project delays and supply-chain bottlenecks can affect delivery timing.

nodes.label: Nuclear Operators & Utilities
nodes.plain_english_description: Electricity generators with significant nuclear baseload capacity or power contracts serving data-centre operators.
nodes.risk_note: Broad utilities without nuclear exposure remain monitor-only.

nodes.label: Uranium Supply
nodes.plain_english_description: Uranium miners and producers that benefit from nuclear power demand growth. Separate from nuclear operators.
nodes.risk_note: Uranium price cycles and long-term contract dynamics create volatility.

nodes.label: Enrichment / HALEU
nodes.plain_english_description: Companies involved in uranium enrichment and High-Assay Low-Enriched Uranium (HALEU) production required by advanced reactor designs.
nodes.risk_note: Requires official source or curated evidence — LLM-only suggestions stay proposed.

nodes.label: Reactor Services / SMR / Advanced Nuclear
nodes.plain_english_description: Companies providing nuclear reactor services, small modular reactor (SMR) technology, and advanced nuclear fuel solutions.
nodes.risk_note: Many SMR companies are speculative and catalyst-driven — route limited.

nodes.label: Copper / Critical Minerals Adjacency (AI Power)
nodes.plain_english_description: Copper miners benefiting from AI power infrastructure and grid expansion — adjacent to nuclear/energy branch but kept separate.
nodes.risk_note: Copper branch should not be mixed with nuclear unless both drivers are active.

nodes.label: Direct GLP-1 Leaders
nodes.plain_english_description: Companies with primary revenue from approved GLP-1 obesity and diabetes drugs.
nodes.risk_note: Valuation and competitive pipeline risk.

nodes.label: Next-Generation Obesity Developers
nodes.plain_english_description: Clinical-stage companies developing next-generation obesity and metabolic treatments. Mostly catalyst-driven; not direct revenue leaders.
nodes.risk_note: Clinical trial results are binary events; high binary risk.

nodes.label: Peptide API, Fill-Finish & Delivery
nodes.plain_english_description: Supply-chain companies providing peptide manufacturing, fill-finish capacity, and injectable delivery systems for GLP-1 drugs.
nodes.risk_note: Supply-chain exposure, not drug revenue exposure.

nodes.label: Diagnostics & Monitoring
nodes.plain_english_description: Diagnostic testing and metabolic monitoring devices with mixed exposure as GLP-1 changes disease management patterns.
nodes.risk_note: Mixed impact — some devices face pressure if GLP-1 reduces insulin/device need.

nodes.label: Healthcare Payers & PBMs
nodes.plain_english_description: Health insurers and pharmacy benefit managers with mixed GLP-1 exposure: higher drug costs vs long-term comorbidity savings.
nodes.risk_note: Direction is unclear — coverage decisions and negotiated pricing create volatility.

nodes.label: Food & Restaurant Pressure Watch
nodes.plain_english_description: Consumer food and restaurant companies facing volume headwinds from GLP-1-driven appetite reduction.
nodes.risk_note: Monitor-only or negative exposure. Never automatic short/long.

nodes.label: Prime Defence Contractors
nodes.plain_english_description: Large-cap prime contractors with multi-year government defence backlogs in missiles, aircraft, submarines, and electronics.
nodes.risk_note: Use order backlog and segment exposure evidence.

nodes.label: Missiles, Air Defence & Munitions
nodes.plain_english_description: Companies producing missile systems, air defence systems, and munitions with direct contract and replenishment demand.
nodes.risk_note: Catalyst-sensitive; requires contract/backlog/news evidence.

nodes.label: Shipbuilding & Naval Systems
nodes.plain_english_description: Companies building US Navy ships, submarines, and naval combat systems.
nodes.risk_note: Use official segment mapping; avoid generic industrials.

nodes.label: Drones, Autonomy & Space/Security Tech
nodes.plain_english_description: Drone manufacturers, autonomous weapons, space reconnaissance, and defence IT companies.
nodes.risk_note: High attention; route as tactical/swing unless structural evidence is strong.

nodes.label: Defence ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Defence-focused ETFs providing basket exposure; holdings are candidate discovery sources, not recommendations.

nodes.label: Platform Security Leaders
nodes.plain_english_description: Large-cap enterprise security platform providers with comprehensive endpoint, network, and firewall capabilities.
nodes.risk_note: Classify by product and segment exposure.

nodes.label: Cloud, SASE & Zero Trust
nodes.plain_english_description: Cloud-native security platforms providing secure access service edge (SASE) and zero-trust network access.
nodes.risk_note: Growth/valuation-sensitive; evidence from company description and revenue mix.

nodes.label: Identity, Privileged Access & Data Security
nodes.plain_english_description: Identity and access management, privileged access management, and data security platforms.
nodes.risk_note: Specific bucket evidence required.

nodes.label: Backup, Recovery & Resilience
nodes.plain_english_description: Data backup, ransomware recovery, and cyber resilience platform providers.
nodes.risk_note: Avoid generic storage names without cyber evidence.

nodes.label: Cyber ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Cybersecurity ETFs providing basket exposure; holdings are candidate discovery sources.

nodes.label: Semiconductor Manufacturing & Equipment
nodes.plain_english_description: Wafer fabrication equipment, process control, and testing companies serving semiconductor fab construction.
nodes.risk_note: Separate fabs/equipment/testing; source with CHIPS/project or company data.

nodes.label: Factory Automation & Industrial Controls
nodes.plain_english_description: Industrial automation systems, process controls, and robotics for domestic manufacturing facilities.
nodes.risk_note: Evidence from company profiles.

nodes.label: Engineering, Construction & Electrical Contractors
nodes.plain_english_description: Engineering, construction, and electrical contractors executing industrial capex and semiconductor fab projects.
nodes.risk_note: Project/backlog sensitive; route by catalyst and infrastructure spend.

nodes.label: Materials, Steel, Cement & Aggregates
nodes.plain_english_description: Steel, cement, and construction materials benefiting from domestic industrial capex cycles.
nodes.risk_note: Cyclical; require price and demand gates.

nodes.label: Industrial & Infrastructure ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Infrastructure ETFs providing basket exposure to US industrial and construction companies.

nodes.label: Homebuilders
nodes.plain_english_description: National and regional homebuilders with direct exposure to mortgage rate changes and housing demand cycles.
nodes.risk_note: Support with permits/starts/order/backlog data.

nodes.label: Building Products & Materials
nodes.plain_english_description: Suppliers of structural building products, insulation, roofing, and interior products to homebuilders.
nodes.risk_note: Sensitive to starts and remodel cycle.

nodes.label: Home Improvement Retail
nodes.plain_english_description: Retailers serving the repair, remodel, and new construction market. Not the same as new construction.
nodes.risk_note: Repair/remodel cycle differs from new construction cycle.

nodes.label: Mortgage, Title & Real Estate Services
nodes.plain_english_description: Mortgage originators, title insurers, and real estate services with direct rate sensitivity.
nodes.risk_note: Rate-sensitive; high volatility and mortgage spread risk.

nodes.label: Housing ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Homebuilder and building industry ETFs providing basket exposure.

nodes.label: Water Utilities
nodes.plain_english_description: Regulated investor-owned water utilities with rate-case driven earnings growth.
nodes.risk_note: Rate-case and geography evidence required.

nodes.label: Treatment, Testing & Analytics
nodes.plain_english_description: Water treatment, testing, and analytical instrument companies. Water-specific evidence required.
nodes.risk_note: Life-science tools do not auto-qualify on water keyword alone.

nodes.label: Pumps, Valves, Meters & Infrastructure
nodes.plain_english_description: Flow control, metering, and infrastructure products for water distribution networks.
nodes.risk_note: Map by product function.

nodes.label: Water ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Water-focused ETFs providing basket exposure; holdings are discovery sources.

nodes.label: Copper Miners
nodes.plain_english_description: Pure-play and primary copper miners with high revenue purity to copper price.
nodes.risk_note: Copper purity score required for diversified miners.

nodes.label: Lithium & Battery Materials
nodes.plain_english_description: Lithium producers and battery material companies. Separate from copper — only activate if battery/EV driver is active.
nodes.risk_note: Do not mix with copper miners unless active driver includes battery materials.

nodes.label: Rare Earths
nodes.plain_english_description: Rare earth miners and processors. Geopolitical supply-chain driver can activate this bucket.
nodes.risk_note: Geopolitical supply-chain driver required for activation.

nodes.label: Copper & Critical Minerals ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Copper and critical minerals ETFs providing basket exposure.

nodes.label: Bullion ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Gold bullion ETPs providing direct price exposure without operating company risk.
nodes.risk_note: Not a company exposure — ETP structure only.

nodes.label: Senior Gold Miners
nodes.plain_english_description: Large-cap gold mining companies with operational leverage to gold price appreciation.
nodes.risk_note: Mine cost and jurisdiction risk required.

nodes.label: Royalty / Streaming
nodes.plain_english_description: Gold and precious metals royalty and streaming companies with lower cost structure than operators.
nodes.risk_note: Different risk profile from miners — should be a separate bucket.

nodes.label: Junior Miners & ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Junior gold miner ETFs providing higher-beta precious metals exposure.
nodes.risk_note: Higher beta; route as ETF route or tactical.

nodes.label: Spot Bitcoin ETP Route
nodes.plain_english_description: SEC-approved spot Bitcoin ETPs providing direct Bitcoin price exposure in a regulated equity wrapper.
nodes.risk_note: ETP route only — no direct operating company exposure.

nodes.label: Crypto Exchanges & Platforms
nodes.plain_english_description: Crypto exchange and platform companies with revenue tied to trading volumes, custody, and subscription services.
nodes.risk_note: Revenue sensitive to trading volume, custody, subscriptions, and regulation.

nodes.label: Corporate Bitcoin Exposure
nodes.plain_english_description: Companies holding Bitcoin on their balance sheet as a treasury reserve asset.
nodes.risk_note: High volatility and leverage/valuation controls required.

nodes.label: Bitcoin Miners
nodes.plain_english_description: Bitcoin mining companies with revenue tied to mining economics, hash price, energy costs, and Bitcoin holdings.
nodes.risk_note: Mining economics, hash price, energy cost, and balance-sheet risk required.

nodes.label: Crypto / Blockchain ETF Route
nodes.plain_english_description: Blockchain and crypto-focused ETFs providing basket exposure; holdings are discovery sources.

nodes.label: Memory & Storage Cycle
nodes.plain_english_description: AI data-centre buildout is driving structural demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM), DRAM, NAND flash, and enterprise storage. Memory pricing is cyclical, but AI training and inference create a demand floor independent of the traditional PC/handset cycle.
nodes.risk_note: Memory is a commodity cycle — oversupply and pricing collapses are the key risk. HBM demand depends on sustained hyperscaler AI capex.

nodes.label: HBM, DRAM, NAND & Enterprise Storage
nodes.plain_english_description: AI data-centre buildout is driving structural demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM), DRAM, NAND flash, and enterprise storage. Memory pricing is cyclical, but AI training and inference create a demand floor independent of the traditional PC/handset cycle.
nodes.risk_note: Memory is a commodity cycle — oversupply and pricing collapses are the key risk. HBM demand depends on sustained hyperscaler AI capex.
